nativeRange.habitat: Coastal protected reefs over rubble or algal-covered rocks, typically at depths of 10–20 m.
nativeRange.biotope: Reef-associated tropical waters from East Africa to Sumatra and adjacent oceanic islands.

diet.notes: The Red Scooter Dragonet is a specialist micropredator that grazes live rock and sand primarily for live copepods and amphipods. Some specimens will accept frozen brine shrimp, bloodworms, and glassworms once they have settled into a new tank, but many individuals initially refuse prepared foods. A well-established refugium or regular pod dosing is generally advised to maintain a sufficient food supply.

avoidWith: fast feeders that outcompete for pods (anthias, wrasses, dragonet-eating dottybacks)

summary: A small, peaceful Indo-Pacific dragonet with intricate red-and-cream stellate markings. Generally considered advanced to keep because of its reliance on live copepods.
careNotes: Red Scooter Dragonets are deliberate, slow-moving micropredators that graze live rock and sand for copepods and amphipods. They are commonly considered difficult because newly-acquired specimens often refuse prepared foods and slowly starve in tanks without an established pod population. Housing in a mature reef system — around 6–8 months established — with abundant live rock and, ideally, an attached refugium continually seeding pods is generally advised. Scaleless skin makes this fish highly sensitive to copper-based medications; quarantine without copper if treatment is needed. Males are strongly intolerant of conspecific males and will generally fight to the death, so keep singly or as one male with 2–3 females introduced simultaneously. The species is widely considered reef-safe with corals and ornamental invertebrates.
breedingNotes: Red Scooter Dragonets are pelagic spawners, meaning eggs and sperm are released into the open water column during a characteristic dusk courtship ascent. Captive breeding has been reported but is generally considered difficult. The primary hurdle is rearing the tiny pelagic larvae, which require appropriately sized live foods during their early development.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (family Callionymidae, order Callionymiformes), maximum size (7.5 cm), native range (East Africa to Sumatra), and wild temperature range (24.6–28.7°C). Designated Reef2Reef wiki secondary returned HTTP 403; swapped to FishBase per playbook fallback.

additional.name: Aquatics Unlimited
additional.notes: Cross-checked tank size (55 gal minimum), water parameters, diet specifics, and intra-specific male aggression. Confirmed copper sensitivity context is implicit (advanced care, scaleless dragonet).

fish.breedingNotes: A pelagic spawner that releases gametes during a dusk courtship ascent. Captive larval rearing is generally considered the limiting factor, as the tiny larvae require appropriately sized live foods.
